task_completion.description: Open and read the audit access policy file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad) or terminal window (e.g., using cat or less) displaying the contents of /home/ga/Documents/audit_access_policy.txt.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Roles and permissions section in Redmine
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the Redmine Administration interface with the 'Roles and permissions' page open.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct role name for the new role
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New role' creation form is visible with 'External Auditor' typed into the 'Name' text field.

task_completion.description: Check the required read-only permissions according to the policy
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for permissions such as 'View project', 'View issue watchers', and 'View wiki edits' are checked in the role creation/edit form.

task_completion.description: Ensure forbidden permissions are not granted
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes for permissions such as 'Add issues', 'Edit issues', 'Delete issues', 'Log time', 'View time', and posting in forums/news are explicitly left unchecked.

task_completion.description: Save the new role and verify it appears in the roles list
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Successful creation') is shown, or the 'Roles and permissions' list displays the newly created 'External Auditor' role.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the Redmine GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) navigating the Redmine web interface, rather than terminal curl/API commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened the policy file to read it
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the file /home/ga/Documents/audit_access_policy.txt being opened and viewed, proving the agent didn't just guess the permissions.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of Python, Ruby, or Bash scripts being executed to interact directly with the Redmine database or API to create the role.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The role to be created must be named exactly 'External Auditor'. The policy file is located at '/home/ga/Documents/audit_access_policy.txt'. The policy mandates specific read-only permissions (including view_project, view_issue_watchers, and view_wiki_edits) and explicitly forbids adding/editing/deleting issues, logging/viewing time, and posting in forums/news. Verify that the agent checks the required permissions and leaves the forbidden ones unchecked.
